violations.description: Person in charge. — --Certified food service manager had an expired certificate or is no longer associated with this establishment.
General employee practices. — --Food employee observed drinking/using open beverage container outside of designated area. Open drinking cup next to single-use items in the kitchen.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Boxes of flatbread being stored on the floor next to the pop machine.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw chicken being stored above raw beef in the meat department cooler.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses.--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Scoop being used for mac & cheese was being stored out on the counter and was still covered in food debris.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Several containers of pork gravy and meats in the reach in coolers were not labeled.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Several countertops and shelves have either chipped edges or are missing their edge trimming. They are no longer an easily cleanable surface. The duct tape on the doors on the hot food display is not a cleanable surface.
Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not designed and/or maintained properly. Hood vents are missing over the donut fryer.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. The soda dispenser needs cleaning.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Several gaskets throughout facility are in need of cleaning. Reach-in coolers need a more thorough cleaning. Inside all of the microwaves need to be cleaned. Walk-in cooler and freezer shelving needs cleaning.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The fryers need more cleaning.
Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in the proper location to protect from contamination. Clean dishes were drying on towels in the dishwashing area.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Boxes of single-use to-go containers were being stored on the floor by the soda dispenser.--Single-service item was reused or used more than one time. Single-use cups were being used to scoop food in the pizza making station.
Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. Soda dispenser was leaking and pooling water underneath on the countertop.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not kept clean or maintained. Hand sink in the kitchen had trash and food debris in it.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. No soap for the hand sink in the meat department.--Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. No disposable towels or air drying device by the hand sink in the meat dept.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors throughout the facility need to be cleaned.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. Missing ceiling tiles in the dish area and over the bread proofer.

Lighting. — --Lighting did not have protective shielding in areas where there is exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, or unwrapped single-service items. Exposed lighting in both the walk-in cooler and freezer.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Employees personal items were being stored on top of food item in the baking area.

Person in charge. — --Certified food service manager had an expired certificate or is no longer associated with this establishment. - The certificate on file is expired and could not verify if the employee had a current certificate.

General employee practices. — --Food employee was observed drinking or using open beverage container outside of designated area.--Food employee was observed eating while preparing food and outside of designated area.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. - Dirty floor matts were placed on top of buckets of icing in the bakery section.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. - Raw meat piece found in a container holding single service items.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution between uses. - Cloths used to clean food surfaces were left out on the counters and not placed in any sanitizing solution between uses.--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. - Bins of nuts and baking products have scoops with their handles placed in the product.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food prepared more than 24 hours ago was not properly date marked. - Several containers of ready to eat products in the reach-in coolers and walk-in coolers were not date marked or labeled properly.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. - Several countertops and shelves have either chipped edges or are missing their edge trimming. They are no longer an easily cleanable surface.
Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not maintained properly. - Missing filters in the hood over the donut fryer.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. - Soda dispenser in the kitchen needs cleaned.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. - Several gaskets throughout facility are in need of cleaning. Reach-in coolers need a more thorough cleaning. - Inside of the microwave with a "lil bubba" sticker on it needs to be cleaned. - Walk-in cooler and freezer shelving needs cleaning.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. - Shelving units, storage units, cabinets, drawers, speed racks, and every piece of kitchen equipment needs a more thorough cleaning.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned as frequently as required. - Hood system over the donut fryer needs cleaned.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in the proper location to protect from contamination. - Storing clean and sanitized dishes on cloth towel next to the dish machine.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. - Boxes of single use items being stored on the floor next to the walk-in freezer. - Uncovered single-use food containers next to the garbage cans were covered in food debris and discarded trash items.
Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not operated or maintained according to regulation. - The soda dispenser was leaking and pooling water on the counter top under the machine.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (floors, walls, ceiling etc) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. - Floors, walls, and ceilings throughout the facility are in need of a more thorough cleaning.
Lighting. — --Lighting did not have protective shielding in areas where there is exposed food. - Both the walk-in cooler and freezer have exposed light fixtures.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not stored in a manner to protect against contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items. Bottles of degreaser and glass cleaner stored with the clean dishes.
Clean dry location, protected (1 pt deducted)
